metadata.title: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men
metadata.study_design: Intravenous infusion of [U-13C]palmitate during prolonged overnight fast (12-19h) in healthy, lean young men and women
metadata.demographics: Healthy, lean young men and women (n=12), matched for age and BMI, free from disease, weight stable, not taking lipid-lowering medication

evidence.quote: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men
Kyriakoula Marinou1,2, Martin Adiels3, Leanne Hodson1, Keith N. Frayn1, Fredrik Karpe1 and Barbara A. Fielding1*
Volunteers were studied during a prolonged overnight fast (12–19h) using an intravenous infusion of [U-13C]palmitate.
Healthy, lean young men and women (n 12) were studied, and matched for age and BMI with no significant difference in waist circumference
Healthy, lean young men and women (n 12) were studied

methods_participants.label: Inclusion criteria - Health status
methods_participants.value: Healthy, lean, weight stable, no disease, no lipid-lowering medication, no medication altering lipid metabolism

evidence.quote: Subjects, recruited from the wider Oxford community via advertisement, were free from any disease, weight stable and not taking any lipid-lowering medication or medication that would alter lipid metabolism.

methods_participants.label: Pre-study dietary restriction
methods_participants.value: Avoid 13C enriched food for 48h, no strenuous exercise or alcohol for 24h, low-fat meal evening before, overnight fast before study day

evidence.quote: Before the study day, subjects were asked to avoid food-stuffs naturally enriched in 13C for 48h
Fat mass was measured by bioelectrical impedance analysis

At time 0, a continuous intravenous infusion (0·04mmol/kg per min) of potassium [U-13C]palmitate (isotope purity 98%; Cambridge Isotope Laboratories, Inc., Andover, MA, USA) complexed to human albumin was started
This was continued until the end of the experiment (at 7h).

methods_participants.label: Plasma metabolite analysis method
methods_participants.value: Sequential flotation using density gradient ultracentrifugation

evidence.quote: plasma NEFA, VLDL-TAG and 3-OHB concentrations were determined enzymatically

methods_participants.label: Menstrual cycle timing
methods_participants.value: Follicular phase (first week of menstrual cycle)

evidence.quote: The studies were performed during the first week (follicular phase) of the menstrual cycle

methods_participants.label: Lipoprotein isolation
methods_participants.method: sequential flotation using density gradient ultracentrifugation

evidence.quote: In ten subjects, VLDL (Svedberg flotation rate (S) 60–400) and VLDL (S 20–60) were isolated by sequential flotation using density gradient ultracentrifugation

narrative_results.statement: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men

evidence.quote: Young women partition fatty acids towards ketone body production rather than VLDL-TAG synthesis, compared with young men

narrative_results.statement: After 12h fasting, women had a more advantageous metabolic profile with lower plasma glucose and TAG but higher plasma NEFA concentrations

evidence.quote: After 12h fasting, the women had a more advantageous metabolic profile with lower plasma glucose (P,0·05) and TAG (P,0·05) but higher plasma NEFA (P,0·05) concentrations

narrative_results.statement: The ratio of 13C-3-OHB to 13C-VLDL-TAG in plasma was significantly higher in women than in men

evidence.quote: The ratio of [13C]3-OHB:[13C]VLDL-TAG in the plasma was significantly higher in women than in men (2·73 (SE 1·7) v. 0·66 (SE 0·30); P¼0·027 for VLDL1 and 3·24 (SE 1·6) v. 0·97 (SE 0·43); P¼0·014 for VLDL2)

narrative_results.statement: There were no sex differences for the incorporation of 13C into VLDL-TAG

evidence.quote: There were no sex differences for the incorporation of 13C into VLDL - or VLDL -TAG

discrepancies.pass_2_value: Pass 1 reports both men and women ages; Pass 2 only reports men's age (25.5 years), omitting women's age (22.5 years)
Pass 1 reports BMI data (Men 21.5, Women 21.0); Pass 2 reports women's age (22.5 years). These are completely different facts
Pass 1 reports only exercise restriction (24 hours); Pass 2 reports both exercise AND alcohol restriction (24 hours). Pass 2 contains additional information not in Pass 1
Pass 1 describes low-fat meal evening before study; Pass 2 describes overnight fast. These are different pre-study protocols
Pass 1 reports fasting period (overnight fast); Pass 2 reports isotope tracer duration (7 hours). These are completely different facts

semantic_groups.rationale: Both describe identical sex distribution (6 men, 6 women) with only minor label variation
Pass 1 reports both men and women ages; Pass 2 only reports men's age (25.5 years), omitting women's age (22.5 years)
Pass 1 reports BMI data (Men 21.5, Women 21.0); Pass 2 reports women's age (22.5 years). These are completely different facts
Both describe identical inclusion criteria with negligible wording differences ('any disease' vs 'disease')
Both report 48-hour pre-study dietary restriction avoiding 13C-enriched foods with minor phrasing differences
Pass 1 reports only exercise restriction (24 hours); Pass 2 reports both exercise AND alcohol restriction (24 hours). Pass 2 contains additional information not in Pass 1
Pass 1 describes low-fat meal evening before study; Pass 2 describes overnight fast. These are different pre-study protocols
Pass 1 reports fasting period (overnight fast); Pass 2 reports isotope tracer duration (7 hours). These are completely different facts
Both describe bioelectrical impedance analysis for body composition. Pass 2 specifies measured parameters (fat mass, fat-free mass) while Pass 1 focuses on method name. Same underlying fact.

Both describe isotope tracer infusion with identical dose (0.04 mmol/kg per min) and route (continuous IV). Pass 1 includes specific tracer name [U-13C]palmitate; Pass 2 omits it. Same fact, minor detail difference.
Pass 1 contains infusion duration data (7 hours); Pass 2 is empty. Only one side present.
Both describe enzymatic determination method for plasma metabolites. Pass 2 adds specific metabolites measured (NEFA, VLDL-TAG, 3-OHB); Pass 1 focuses on method. Same underlying fact.
Both identify RIA as insulin measurement method. Pass 2 specifies measured parameter (plasma insulin concentrations); Pass 1 focuses on method. Same fact.
Both describe sequential flotation using density gradient ultracentrifugation for VLDL isolation. Pass 2 includes sample size (10 subjects) which is supplementary information. Same method.
Both identify GC and GC-MS for fatty acid analysis. Pass 2 adds isotopic enrichment measurement detail; Pass 1 focuses on analytical methods. Same underlying analytical approach.
Both specify follicular phase of menstrual cycle. Pass 1 adds clarification (first week); Pass 2 is more concise. Negligible wording difference for same fact.
Both extractions report identical participant count of 12 with identical formatting
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
Same fact expressed with slightly different clause structure; 'but this was not observed in men' vs 'but in men, this was not observed'
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
Identical text across both extractions
